State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 607 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322 5662          322 5660          322 5901          322 6441          322 6444

March 11, 1981

Leland H. Jordan
Bagshaw, Martinelli, Corrigan & Jordan
Suite 303, Citizens Building
950 Northgate Drive
San Rafael, CA 94903

Dear Mr. Jordan:

Mr. Blaiser has referred your letter of January 9, 1981, to me. Ms. Cameron, whom you discussed this matter with on several prior occasions, is no longer with the Commission and has also transferred her files on the matter to me. I have reviewed the files and shall now attempt to respond to your inquiries.

First, you have asked whether or not Mr. Smith can continue to serve on the San Rafael Planning Commission. The Political Reform Act only requires disqualification in the case of those individual decisions in which a public official has a financial interest. Government Code Section 87100. Section 87100 does not provide for removal of a public official from his official position. Whether a situation could arise in which disqualification would be required so frequently that a local official could not adequately perform his or her duties is not a question for the Commission.

You have also asked advice on some specific situations. However, at this point I am not clear as to which matters still require decision by the Planning Commission, and which have already been decided. The Commission does not give advice on decisions which already have been made. If an official has acted improperly in connection with a past decision, there are, of course, adequate remedies under the Act. Sections 83116, 91000-91014.

At this point, I would like you to let me know exactly what future decisions Mr. Smith wishes advice about. I will then review the materials related to that decision which

Leland H. Jordan
March 11, 1981
Page Two


have been provided by you and by Mr. Duggan. If I need any
additional information, I will let you know. If necessary,
we can arrange to have you and possibly Mr. Duggan come to
the FPPC to discuss the issues raised. Once we narrow the
request for advice to specific questions concerning future
decisions, I think that we should be able to provide you
with a prompt response.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief Counsel
Legal Division

BAM:kp

BAGSHAW, MARTINELLI, CORRIGAN & JORDAN
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

RODERICK P. MARTINELLI
WILLIAM G CORRIGAN
LELAND H JORDAN
WILLIAM S HOCHMAN
RICHARD L SOLOMON,JR.
STEPHEN P McGEE
DANIEL D JORDAN

SUITE 303 CITIZENS BUILDING
950 NORTHGATE DRIVE

A.C.BAGSHAW
OF COUNSEL

SAN RAFAEL, CALIFORNIA 94903

TELEPHONE (415) 472-4500

January 9, 1981


Mr. Robert D. Blasier, Jr.
Director
Enforcement Division
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Planning Commissioner Michael Smith;
_____ FPPC No. GC-80/08-20

Dear Mr. Blasier:

I have your letter of January 6, 1981. Apparently, there
is some confusion on this matter.

Some time shortly prior to October 10, 1980, I had a tele-
phone conversation with Ms. Sarah Cameron of your office in
which I requested that the FPPC issue an opinion relative to
the duties of Michael Smith as a member of the San Rafael
Planning Commission. That request related to the specific
question raised by the City Attorney's letter of August 19,
1980, addressed to the FPPC.

In my telephone conversation with Sarah Cameron, she requested
additional information from me. On October 13, 1980, I
addressed a letter to Ms. Cameron, copy enclosed, in which
I advised her that I would attempt to obtain the additional
data which she requested.

On October 31, 1980, I addressed a letter to Sarah Cameron
(copy enclosed) together with a letter addressed to my
Michael Smith dated October 28, 1980 (copy enclosed). Mr.
Smith's letter included the information which Ms. Cameron
had requested. In that letter, I asked that my client and
I be afforded the opportunity to come to Sacramento to
discuss the matter with the FPPC staff. I have heard noth-
ing further from the FPPC.

As indicated in my letter of October 31, 1980, the City
Attorney has now made a general attack on Mr. Smith's
right to serve as a member of the San Rafael Planning Com-
mission. Because of the repeated public attacks on Mr.
Smith by the City Attorney, I filed an action in Marin

Badshaw, Martinelli Corrigan & Jordan

to Mr. Robert D. Blasier, Jr.                    DATE  1/9/81      PAGE  2


     Superior Court on December 8, 1980, seeking a clarification
of Mr. Smith's right to serve on the Planning Commission,
and also seeking a declaratory judgment relating to the most
recent specific challenge made by the City Attorney. A copy
of my complaint in that matter is enclosed. On December 11,
1980, I addressed a letter to the Mayor of San Rafael, out-
lining my position on this latest question and explaining
the reasons for the commencement of litigation. I assume
that is the memorandum referred to in your letter to me
dated January 6, 1981. If not, a copy of that letter is
enclosed.

     We again solicit an opinion of the FPPC regarding Mr. Smith's
entitlement to serve as a San Rafael Planning Commissioner
and his right to participate in the specific issues referred
to in the various documents now in your possession.

     We are quite willing, as suggested in your letter of January 6,
1981, to seek the Commission's advise on future votes which
may be called under question.  However, this may create a
practical problem. We are unable to foretell the occasions
when the City Attorney will challenge Mr. Smith's right to
vote. Although the City Attorney has advised me unequivocally
that there is an inherent conflict which precludes Mr. Smith
from serving as a member of the Planning Commission, he has
chosen to challenge his votes only on a selective basis. Mr.
Smith cannot afford to have independent legal counsel in
attendance at all Planning Commission meetings in view of
the City Attorney's conclusion that a fundamental conflict
disqualifies Mr. Smith from acting on any questions, it is
only natural that Mr. Smith has little faith in the City
Attorney's judgment on this issue. Consequently, if specific
challenges are not raised until the Planning Commission is
actually in session, Mr. Smith has no opportunity to consult
either me or the FPPC. The Planning Commission agendas are
available to the City Attorney several days prior to the
meetings. If the City Attorney will advise me when he receives
these agendas of any issue on which he intends to challenge
Mr. Smith's participation, I will be happy to discuss it with
your staff in advance. I do not see any other way we can
operate.

                                        Yours sincerely,


                                        [signature]
                                        LELAND H. JORDAN

LHJ:ac
Enclosures

cc:  (see attached listing)

BAGSHAW, MARTINELLI, CORRIGAN & JORDAN

TO Mr. Robert D. Blasier, Jr.                DATE  1/9/81        PAGE  3


CC:  The Honorable Lawrence E. Mulryan
     Mayor of the City of San Rafael

     Mr. Jack G. Nixon
     Member of the City Council

     Mr. John A. Miskimen
     Member of the City Council

     Mrs. Dorothy Breiner
     Member of the City Council

     Mr. Fred Jensen
     Member of the City Council

     Mr. John Starkweather
     Chairman, Planning Commission

     Mr. Robert S. Beyer
     City Manager

     Mr. Edmund A. Duggan
     City Attorney

     Mr. William T. Bullard, Jr.
     Assistant City Attorney

     Mr. Michael Smith
     Post Office Box 3829
     San Rafael, California  94902
